title: The Riddle
content:
I

    Stretching eyes west
    Over the sea,
    Wind foul or fair,
    Always stood she
    Prospect-impressed;
    Solely out there
    Did her gaze rest,
    Never elsewhere
    Seemed charm to be.

II

    Always eyes east
    Ponders she now -
    As in devotion -
    Hills of blank brow
    Where no waves plough.
    Never the least
    Room for emotion
    Drawn from the ocean
    Does she allow.
meaning:
"The Riddle" is a quintessential example of Thomas Hardy's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
